message:
feat(messaging): obligation-backed consumer delivery with redelivery, dead-letter, and stats

Integrate FabricConsumer with the ObligationLedger so every dispatched
delivery is backed by a tracked obligation that must be committed
(acknowledged) or aborted (nacked/dead-lettered/superseded).

New types and fields:
- FabricConsumerOwner: TaskId+RegionId ownership anchor for obligations
- PendingAckState gains request, supersedes_obligation_id fields
- AttemptCertificate gains supersedes_obligation_id field
- FabricConsumer gains ledger: ObligationLedger, owner: FabricConsumerOwner

New methods on FabricConsumer:
- redeliver_delivery(): abort the prior attempt's obligation and mint a
  fresh one, linking them via supersedes_obligation_id for audit trail
- dead_letter_delivery(): abort the attempt's obligation with Error reason
  and record the dead-letter reason for the coverage map
- obligation_stats(): expose LedgerStats (acquired/committed/aborted/pending)

Behavioral changes:
- dispatch_push() now reserves an obligation for each delivery
- acknowledge_delivery() commits the backing obligation on success
- Stale ack on a superseded obligation returns StaleNoOp without error

Tests added:
- fabric_consumer_redelivery_mints_new_obligation_and_supersedes_old_attempt
- fabric_consumer_dead_letter_records_reason_and_aborts_obligation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
